meta_title: For Freelancers: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Solve Debugging Bottlenecks for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to use AI-driven design workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress your clients today.
content.answer_block: Stop manually chasing syntax errors. By shifting your workflow from 'coding first' to 'designing with AI,' you treat bugs as architectural constraints rather than failures. Use Antigravity to map your state logic and Claude Code to auto-refactor the implementation, ensuring a resilient, production-ready codebase in a fraction of the time.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank terminal, the cursor blinking like a countdown timer. Your client's deadline is looming, and that elusive race condition in the state management layer is refusing to yield. You have spent hours chasing logs, feeling less like a developer and more like a janitor cleaning up your own mess. This is the silent killer of freelance momentum: the debugging void.

But what if you stopped debugging and started designing? By leveraging AI-first workflows, you detach yourself from the syntax-level grind and move into the role of an architect. When you design with AI, you build structural integrity into the code from the start, rendering traditional debugging sessions obsolete. It is time to reclaim your billable hours.

steps.title: Define the Architectural Constraint
steps.description: Before writing code, visualize the data flow using AI to define structural boundaries. This prevents the 'spaghetti state' that causes 90% of bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this feature requirement. Create a high-level state machine diagram that handles error states and edge cases explicitly. Focus on strict data validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a state machine schema for the user authentication flow based on the provided requirements'

steps.title: Generate Scaffold with Type Safety
steps.description: Use AI to generate the skeleton of your application with strict types. Type-safe code is self-documenting and significantly easier to debug.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular TypeScript boilerplate for this design, including interfaces for all API response objects and state transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file src/types.ts --prompt 'Generate strict interfaces for the user profile service'

steps.title: Implement Logic via AI-Assisted Iteration
steps.description: Feed your scaffold into the AI to write the implementation. This ensures the logic adheres to the architectural design created in step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the authentication service using the previously defined state machine. Use clean, functional programming patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement the auth service logic in src/services/auth.ts using the defined state machine'

steps.title: Automated Contextual Testing
steps.description: Instead of manual debugging, use AI to generate unit tests that cover the boundary conditions defined in your design.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write comprehensive unit tests for the auth service, specifically targeting the error states defined in the state machine.

steps.title: Refine and Optimize
steps.description: Perform a final architectural review with AI to identify potential bottlenecks before the client sees the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a code review on this implementation. Identify any potential race conditions or memory leaks based on the current architecture.

pro_tips: Always define your 'Error State' first; if you know how your app fails, you can design the recovery flow before writing a single line of business logic.
Treat your AI prompts as documentation; keep a 'Prompt Library' for recurring patterns to ensure consistency across multiple client projects.
Use Claude Code's 'review' flag during the CI/CD phase to catch architectural drift before it becomes a technical debt nightmare.
If the AI struggles with a bug, feed it the entire directory context rather than just the file; AI needs to understand the system's 'neighborhood' to debug effectively.
Apply the 'Smallest Unit' rule: force your AI to break complex tasks into 20-line modules; it is mathematically easier for LLMs to generate bug-free code in small chunks.
content.next_steps: Begin by applying this workflow to a non-critical module in your current project. Once you see the reduction in debugging time, move your entire CI/CD pipeline to an AI-augmented review process.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start commanding it. By integrating these tools, you will spend less time in the debugger and more time delivering high-value work to your clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
